IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexBlendModelView

This method is a faster alternative to IDirect3DDevice8::SetTransform, and it works with push-buffer fix-ups.

HRESULT SetVertexBlendModelView(
  UINT Count,
  CONST D3DMATRIX *pModelViews, 
  CONST D3DMATRIX *pInverseModelView,
  CONST D3DMATRIX *pProjectionViewport
);

Parameters

Count
The number of transforms specified.
pModelViews
[in] Pointer to the model-view transform.
pInverseModelView
[in] Pointer to the inverse of the model-view transform. This may be NULL if lighting and texture generation are disabled.
pProjectionViewport
[in] Pointer to the current projection/viewport matrix. This matrix can be obtained using IDirect3DDevice8::GetProjectionViewportMatrix. This matrix changes whenever
  • IDirect3DDevice8::SetTransform is called with D3DTS_PROJECTION.
  • IDirect3DDevice8::SetViewport is called.
  • IDirect3DDevice8::SetRenderState is called using D3DRS_ZENABLE and D3DZB_USEW.
  • Any antialiasing parameters are modified.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

Remarks

This method can only be called while D3DRS_VERTEXBLEND is TRUE. It also cannot be called while in D3DSCM_192CONSTANTS mode because it would overwrite some of the negative shader constants.

When IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexBlendModelView is called with a non-NULL pModelView pointer, the method takes control of the GPU's transform registers until IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexBlendModelView is called with a NULL pModelView pointer. While the function has control, any calls to IDirect3DDevice8::SetTransform will be effectively ignored.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.
